evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed layout clearly separates 4 communities with distinct colors; intra-community density and cross-community bridges are visible; node labels (white on colored nodes) are readable.
evidence.typography: Script sets neither pdf.fonttype=42 nor svg.fonttype=0; PDF contains Type3 (rasterized/bitmap) fonts and DejaVu Sans — the default matplotlib font — rather than an editable outline font.
evidence.palette: Uses a 4-color qualitative palette (#E64B35, #4DBBD5, #00A087, #3C5488) that is reasonably colorblind-friendly (NPG-style palette); no directional color misuse since this is a categorical encoding; red is used for community 0 without conflating it with a directional meaning, which is acceptable here.
evidence.layout_density: 6×6 inch figure is well-filled; Fruchterman-Reingold layout with tuned k-value separates clusters without excess whitespace; legend placed in lower-right without overlapping nodes; no clipped or overlapping labels visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Title and legend present; caption matches figure content; however no node-count or edge-count annotation appears directly on the figure (only in caption), and absence of fonttype=42 means text is not selectable in the PDF — a common reviewer complaint.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is generated but contains Type3 bitmap fonts (DejaVu Sans encoded as Type3), meaning text is not selectable or scalable as true vector text; fonttype=42 is not set in gen_figure.py.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (17 KB), figure.png (101 KB), figure.svg (49 KB), caption.tex (374 B), gen_figure.py present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states node count (30), edge count (68), community count (4), algorithm (Fruchterman-Reingold, NetworkX, seed=42), and describes both intra-community density and cross-community bridges — a specific, informative finding.

overall_strengths: Clear visual separation of community structure with a well-tuned force-directed layout and colorblind-aware qualitative palette.
Detailed, quantitative caption that names the algorithm, parameters, node/edge counts, and describes the key structural observation.
overall_weaknesses: PDF contains Type3 rasterized fonts (DejaVu Sans default) instead of editable Type42/TrueType outlines; pdf.fonttype=42 is missing from the script, making text non-selectable in the PDF.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used rather than a publication-quality font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif); this is a common rejection point for journal submissions.
